channel.title: The Grant Desk - Tech & Infrastructure, added in last 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Tech & Infrastructure, added in last 30 days.

item.title: Padimai: OOPS Onchain Artist Residency 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Padimai Art and Tech Studio

Location: Remote residency (Padimai is based in Singapore); open internationally.

Award: SGD 15,000 honorarium per artist plus an additional SGD 5,000 per artist for documentation (audio, video, photography). Five practitioners selected. No application fee.

Duration: 3 months (20 September to 20 December 2026)

Deadline: 15 Aug 2026

OOPS (Origin Of Primary Source) is the first edition of Padimai Art and Tech Studio's Onchain Artist Residency, a three-month remote residency inviting art practitioners to critically and creatively engage with NFTs as artistic registers and as systems of knowledge, documentation, and access. Five practitioners are selected. SUPPORT: SGD 15,000 honorarium per artist, plus SGD 5,000 per artist for documentation. The residency is remote, with community engagement designed through online meetings; at the end, participants deliver three NFTs developed during the residency to Padimai. ELIGIBILITY: individual creative practitioners working at the intersection of art, technology, and blockchain; open internationally. RESIDENCY: 20 September to 20 December 2026. DEADLINE: 15 August 2026. Apply via the residency page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: America On Tech: Tech Flex Leaders Program
item.description:
Organisation: America On Tech

Location: United States. Remote/hybrid program.

Award: USD 500 stipend on completion, plus an America On Tech certificate. No application fee.

Duration: Program cohort (dates per program).

Deadline: 17 Aug 2026

America On Tech's Tech Flex Leaders Program is open to young people interested in tech who are curious, resourceful, and ready to build something new. No prior tech experience is required. Participants earn an America On Tech certificate and receive a USD 500 stipend for completing the program. WHO IT'S FOR: young people interested in tech; no prior tech experience required. AWARD: USD 500 stipend plus certificate. DEADLINE: 5 July 2026 priority, open until 17 August 2026. TO APPLY: submit the application via the America On Tech program page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Technology Fellow: Digital Safety and Security
item.description:
Organisation: Ford Foundation

Location: New York, New York, USA

Award: USD 166,000 to 170,000 annual salary, plus comprehensive benefits and retirement contributions of up to 13%

Duration: Two-year term-limited contract

Deadline: Closed 06 Jul 2026

The Ford Foundation seeks a two-year Technology Fellow for Digital Safety and Security to expand the foundation's ability to support grantee digital safety and security. Civil society organizations face targeted information security threats yet many lack the resources and training to maintain robust digital security. Reporting to the Director, Office of the Vice President for U.S. Programs, the Fellow will identify field-building opportunities in the public-interest digital safety and security field, work directly with program officers across US and global teams to integrate digital security capacity into Ford's technical assistance programs, provide strategic support to high-risk grantees, and assess strategic gaps in the ecosystem for safety and security providers. The Technology Fellows Program is administered by Ford's Technology and Society Program and is designed to increase sociotechnical capacity within the Foundation. Requirements include a minimum of 7 years' professional experience with media, technology, advocacy or policy organizations; a baccalaureate degree or equivalent experience; sophisticated knowledge of existing and emerging technologies with a focus on surveillance technologies and organizational digital safety and security; and experience designing, implementing or supporting digital safety and security solutions in a complex global setting. Hybrid role based in New York (three days per week in office) with approximately 10 to 20 percent domestic and international travel. Not eligible for visa sponsorship; candidates must be authorized to work in the United States. Submit both a resume and a cover letter to be considered.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Circuit Breakers '26: Tech Worker Conference - Call for Proposals (New York City)
item.description:
Organisation: Tech Workers Coalition

Location: New York City, USA. In-person conference 17-18 October 2026. Open to workers across the tech sector, from software engineers and salespeople to warehouse workers, ride-share drivers and contractors.

Award: No funding. This is a free call for talk/session proposals at a volunteer-organised tech labour conference, not a grant. No submission fee.

Duration: Two-day conference, 17-18 October 2026.

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Circuit Breakers '26 is a tech worker conference organised by volunteers from across the tech labour movement, bringing together workers, organisers and activists in the tech industry to learn, build community and strategise. Tech Workers Coalition defines tech workers broadly, from software engineers and salespeople to warehouse workers and ride-share drivers. The conference centres rank-and-file organising, with workers sharing stories, running skills trainings and leading debates. The open call for proposals welcomes sessions on all aspects of labour organising in tech, with particular interest in three areas: Labor Organizing Against Imperialism; Tech Workers Taking on Technology (layoffs, surveillance, worker control over AI and automation); and Bold Strategy (solidarity, membership structures, return-to-office actions, cross-border organising). Sessions can be proposed via a short form (about 10 minutes). New York City, 17-18 October 2026. This is not a grant and carries no funding; it is a free call for conference talks. NOTE: the call-for-proposals deadline was not yet published at time of listing; confirm the submission deadline and form link on the Circuit Breakers page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Project Liberty: Co-Lab Fund Member Collaboration Grants (Round 1, 2026)
item.description:
Organisation: Project Liberty (Alliance SteerCo); pooled fund supported by Future of Life Institute, Omidyar Network, Ergo Impact, and Project Liberty

Location: Members of the Project Liberty coalition (Alliance). Projects led by a Coalition member organization; geography otherwise unrestricted.

Award: Grants cap at USD 250,000 per project; most first-round awards fall between USD 25,000 and USD 100,000. Pooled fund supported by Future of Life Institute, Omidyar Network, Ergo Impact, and Project Liberty. No application fee.

Duration: Project length varies. Funding rounds run monthly until December 2026, or until the funds are disbursed; decisions are expected within roughly three weeks of each deadline.

Deadline: Closed 21 Jun 2026

MEMBERS ONLY: this is a member-led collaboration fund, not an open public call. Projects must be led by a Project Liberty Coalition (Alliance) member organization. The Co-Lab Fund, launched by the coalition's Steering Committee (SteerCo), supports projects that Coalition members undertake together, building on the trust and relationships that already exist within the community so that funding decisions can rest on the strength of the idea, the people behind it, and their track records rather than extensive diligence. In exchange for a streamlined process, grants stay modest and funded teams share their progress with the broader Coalition as projects develop, creating accountability and letting the community learn from what emerges. Two kinds of projects qualify: (1) work that advances the Coalition's active workstreams (listed in the application), and (2) investment in the Coalition's connective infrastructure, such as shared services and resources that strengthen collective capacity. Eligibility: projects must be led by a Coalition member organization, produce value for more than one Coalition organization, begin substantive work within a few weeks of approval, and fall within the active workstreams; full eligibility details are in the Overview and FAQ document. How it works: monthly rounds are planned until December 2026 or until the funds are disbursed; the application is designed to take an hour or less once the project's shape is clear, and decisions are expected within about three weeks of each deadline. This is the first cycle and the Fund is being built as it goes, so the process is expected to improve each round. Round 1 closes Sunday, 21 June 2026; further rounds follow, so there is no need to force an idea into this cycle. Apply via the Fillout application form.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: TechCongress: Congressional Innovation Fellowship 2027 (Senior and Early-Career)
item.description:
Organisation: TechCongress

Location: Washington, DC, United States. Full-time, in-person; remote participation is not possible. Nonpartisan: fellows serve in both Democratic and Republican offices.

Award: Senior (mid-career) Fellows: USD 100,600/year stipend. Early-career Fellows: USD 78,000 annual-equivalent stipend (USD 6,500/month). Plus health insurance supplement up to USD 425/month, up to USD 2,000 relocation to DC, up to USD 2,000 first-month short-term housing reimbursement, up to USD 2,000 official travel, and a USD 500 professional-attire allowance. No application fee.

Duration: Senior Fellows serve January to December 2027 (optional one-month extension). Early-career Fellows serve January to October 2027.

Deadline: Closed 26 Jun 2026

TechCongress places technologists in the US Congress to build technology capacity among Members and Congressional staff, where fewer than 3% of legislative staff have a technical background. For the January 2027 cohort it is recruiting up to twenty fellows (10 for Democratic offices, 10 for Republican offices) across two tiers: the Senior Congressional Innovation Fellowship (mid-career, at least eight years of work or postgraduate study) and the Congressional Innovation Fellowship (early-career, two to six years of experience, including a recently finished or projected-by-January-2027 graduate technical degree such as a Masters or PhD; applicants with seven years qualify for an intermediate tier). Fellows choose a placement with a Member of Congress or a Congressional Committee, report to a senior staffer (such as a Chief of Staff or Staff Director), and do work similar to other Congressional staff: researching tech policy (cyber and election security, data and biometric privacy, AI policy, autonomous vehicles, health IT, encryption, disinformation), educating Members and staff, writing legislation, preparing Committee hearings, markups and investigations, and building coalitions. No prior government experience is required; the fellowship is first and foremost an educational experience. This year TechCongress also seeks at least two fellows from non-traditional verticals: climate (grid modernization, clean energy, NEPA reform), government innovation (govtech and digital benefits delivery, civil service reform), and science, research and innovation (NSF, NIH, DoE, FDA reform, clinical trials). Technical degrees or experience in engineering, development, journalism, scientific, legal or government roles are encouraged. TechCongress is a nonpartisan nonprofit incubated at the Open Technology Institute at New America; since 2016 it has sent 140 fellows to Congress. Equal opportunity employer. Application deadline: 26 June 2026, 5:59am (applicant's local timezone). Contact: aleena@techcongress.io. Apply via the official application form.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
